title: Solving Equations of the Form $|f(x)| = g(x)$ — Hard

questions.question: Solve the equation $|0.5x + 4| = |1.5x - 2|$.
questions.explanation:
Case 1: $0.5x+4 = 1.5x-2 \implies x=6$. Case 2: $0.5x+4 = -1.5x+2 \implies 2x=-2 \implies x=-1$.

**Common mistake:** Errors in decimal arithmetic or failing to multiply the entire equation by 2 to clear the decimals.

questions.question: Find the values of x such that $| \frac{x+1}{2} | = | \frac{x-3}{4} |$.
questions.explanation:
Multiply by 4: $|2x+2| = |x-3|$. Case 1: $2x+2 = x-3 \implies x=-5$. Case 2: $2x+2 = -x+3 \implies 3x=1 \implies x=1/3$.

**Common mistake:** Failing to multiply both terms in the numerator when clearing the denominators.

questions.question: Determine the solution to $|2x + 1| = |2x + 5|$.
questions.explanation:
Case 1: $2x+1 = 2x+5$ (No solution). Case 2: $2x+1 = -(2x+5) \implies 2x+1 = -2x-5 \implies 4x=-6 \implies x=-1.5$.

**Common mistake:** Incorrectly concluding there is no solution because the x-terms in Case 1 cancel out.

questions.question: Solve the equation $|3(x - 2)| = |2x + 4|$.
questions.explanation:
$|3x-6| = |2x+4|$. Case 1: $3x-6 = 2x+4 \implies x=10$. Case 2: $3x-6 = -2x-4 \implies 5x=2 \implies x=0.4$.

**Common mistake:** Failing to expand $3(x-2)$ before starting the case-by-case solution.

questions.question: Find the points of intersection of the graphs $y = |x - 1|$ and $y = |2x + 4|$.
questions.explanation:
Solve $|x-1| = |2x+4|$. Case 1: $x-1 = 2x+4 \implies x=-5, y=6$. Case 2: $x-1 = -2x-4 \implies 3x=-3, x=-1, y=2$.

**Common mistake:** Providing only the x-coordinates instead of the full coordinates of the intersection points.
